title: EVG
subtitle: A sandbox board-style system with layered mechanics and world structures
description: A sandbox-style board system combining structured world spaces with flexible gameplay systems.
Designed to support evolving mechanics, entities, and layered game interactions.
features: World - Dimension - Biome - Chunk - Space structure
Supports both structured and sandbox interactions

progressNotes: Concept and board game design (2015 to 2018)
Initial idea created as a physical board game.
Core mechanics and gameplay concepts explored manually.
Focused on rules, interactions, and player flow.
Early digital prototype in Visual Basic (2019)
Began translating board game mechanics into digital form.
Created first functional systems and interactions.
Tested feasibility of digital gameplay structure.
Python transition and first working build (2020 to 2021)
Rebuilt the project in Python for better flexibility.
Developed a fully working version of the game.
Established core systems and gameplay loop.
Initial digital beta testing (2021 to 2022)
Ran first digital beta tests.
Collected feedback on mechanics and usability.
Identified structural and scalability issues.
Rebuild phase and system iteration (2022 to 2024)
Rebuilt the system multiple times to improve structure.
Refined core mechanics and internal architecture.
Worked toward more scalable and modular systems.
Modular retrofit system and logging (2025 to Present)
Implemented retrofit definitions and logging systems.
Enabled updating individual classes and systems without full rebuilds.
Improved maintainability and long-term scalability.
Integrated external assistance tools to accelerate development.
